summary:
All tests pass. Here's a summary of the needle and the fix:

**The Bug:** In `/etc/nginx/sites-available/default`, the nginx reverse proxy for `/api/` was pointing to the wrong port:

```nginx
proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:5001;  # ❌ Wrong port
```

**The Fix:** Changed it to match the actual port Flask listens on (`5000`):

```nginx
proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:5000;  # ✅ Correct port
```

The `/health` endpoint always returned 200 because it was handled directly by nginx as a static response (no upstream needed), which masked the misconfiguration. Only `/api/data` actually proxied to the Flask backend — and it was hitting the wrong port (5001 instead of 5000), causing a connection failure.
